modules.description:
gunshi cli entry point.

This entry point exports the bellow APIs and types:
- `cli`: The main CLI function to run the command, included `global options` and `usage renderer` built-in plugins.
- `define`: A function to define a command.
- `defineWithTypes`: A function to define a command with specific type parameters.
- `lazy`: A function to lazily load a command.
- `lazyWithTypes`: A function to lazily load a command with specific type parameters.
- `plugin`: A function to create a plugin.
- `createCommandContext`: A function to create a command context, mainly for testing purposes.
- `args-tokens` utilities, `parseArgs` and `resolveArgs` for parsing command line arguments.
- Some basic type definitions, such as `CommandContext`, `Plugin`, `PluginContext`, etc.

entries.name: cli
entries.description:
Run the command.

This overload accepts any command-like object using a loose structural type.
It bypasses TypeScript contravariance issues with callback properties.

Note: This overload MUST be last in the overload list. TypeScript checks overloads
in declaration order and selects the first matching one. The SubCommandable type
is intentionally loose and would match any command, so placing it first would
prevent proper type inference for more specific command types.

params.name: entry
params.description: A command-like object (command, command runner, or lazy command)

returns.description: A rendered usage or undefined. if you will use {@linkcode CliOptions.usageSilent} option, it will return rendered usage string.

entries.signature: export async function cli(args: string[], entry: SubCommandable, options?: CliOptions): Promise<string | undefined>

entries.name: Args
entries.description:
An object that contains {@link ArgSchema | argument schema}.

This type is used to define the structure and validation rules for command line arguments.
entries.signature: interface Args

entries.name: ArgSchema
entries.description:
An argument schema definition for command-line argument parsing.

This schema is similar to the schema of Node.js `util.parseArgs` but with extended features:
- Additional `required` and `description` properties
- Extended `type` support: 'string', 'boolean', 'number', 'enum', 'positional', 'custom'
- Simplified `default` property (single type, not union types)
examples:
Basic string argument:
```ts
const schema: ArgSchema = {
  type: 'string',
  description: 'Server hostname',
  default: 'localhost'
}
```

Required number argument with alias:
```ts
const schema: ArgSchema = {
  type: 'number',
  short: 'p',
  description: 'Port number to listen on',
  required: true
}
```

Enum argument with choices:
```ts
const schema: ArgSchema = {
  type: 'enum',
  choices: ['info', 'warn', 'error'],
  description: 'Logging level',
  default: 'info'
}
```

members.name: type
members.description:
Type of the argument value.

- `'string'`: Text value (default if not specified)
- `'boolean'`: `true`/`false` flag (can be negatable with `--no-` prefix)
- `'number'`: Numeric value (parsed as integer or float)
- `'enum'`: One of predefined string values (requires `choices` property)
- `'positional'`: Non-option argument by position
- `'custom'`: Custom parsing with user-defined `parse` function

members.name: short
members.description:
Single character alias for the long option name.

As example, allows users to use `-x` instead of `--extended-option`.
Only valid for non-positional argument types.

members.name: description
members.description:
Human-readable description of the argument's purpose.

Used for help text generation and documentation.
Should be concise but descriptive enough to understand the argument's role.

members.name: required
members.description:
Marks the argument as required.

When `true`, the argument must be provided by the user.
If missing, an `ArgResolveError` with type 'required' will be thrown.

Note: Only `true` is allowed (not `false`) to make intent explicit.

members.name: multiple
members.description:
Allows the argument to accept multiple values.

When `true`, the resolved value becomes an array.
For options: can be specified multiple times (--tag foo --tag bar)
For positional: collects remaining positional arguments

Note: Only `true` is allowed (not `false`) to make intent explicit.

members.name: negatable
members.description:
Enables negation for boolean arguments using `--no-` prefix.

When `true`, allows users to explicitly set the boolean to `false`
using `--no-option-name`. When `false` or omitted, only positive
form is available.

Only applicable to `type: 'boolean'` arguments.

members.name: choices
members.description:
Array of allowed string values for enum-type arguments.

Required when `type: 'enum'`. The argument value must be one of these choices,
otherwise an `ArgResolveError` with type 'type' will be thrown.

Supports both mutable arrays and readonly arrays for type safety.

members.name: default
members.description:
Default value used when the argument is not provided.

The type must match the argument's `type` property:
- `string` type: string default
- `boolean` type: boolean default
- `number` type: number default
- `enum` type: must be one of the `choices` values
- `positional`/`custom` type: any appropriate default

members.name: toKebab
members.description:
Converts the argument name from camelCase to kebab-case for CLI usage.

When `true`, a property like `maxCount` becomes available as `--max-count`.
This allows [CAC](https://github.com/cacjs/cac) user-friendly property names while maintaining CLI conventions.

Can be overridden globally with `resolveArgs({ toKebab: true })`.

Note: Only `true` is allowed (not `false`) to make intent explicit.

members.name: conflicts
members.description:
Names of other options that conflict with this option.

When this option is used together with any of the conflicting options,
an `ArgResolveError` with type 'conflict' will be thrown.

Conflicts only need to be defined on one side - if option A defines a conflict

regardless of whether B also defines a conflict with A.

entries.name: Args
entries.description:
An object that contains {@link ArgSchema | argument schema}.

This type is used to define the structure and validation rules for command line arguments.
entries.signature: interface Args

entries.name: ArgSchema
entries.description:
An argument schema definition for command-line argument parsing.

This schema is similar to the schema of Node.js `util.parseArgs` but with extended features:
- Additional `required` and `description` properties
- Extended `type` support: 'string', 'boolean', 'number', 'enum', 'positional', 'custom'
- Simplified `default` property (single type, not union types)
examples:
Basic string argument:
```ts
const schema: ArgSchema = {
  type: 'string',
  description: 'Server hostname',
  default: 'localhost'
}
```

Required number argument with alias:
```ts
const schema: ArgSchema = {
  type: 'number',
  short: 'p',
  description: 'Port number to listen on',
  required: true
}
```

Enum argument with choices:
```ts
const schema: ArgSchema = {
  type: 'enum',
  choices: ['info', 'warn', 'error'],
  description: 'Logging level',
  default: 'info'
}
```

members.name: type
members.description:
Type of the argument value.

- `'string'`: Text value (default if not specified)
- `'boolean'`: `true`/`false` flag (can be negatable with `--no-` prefix)
- `'number'`: Numeric value (parsed as integer or float)
- `'enum'`: One of predefined string values (requires `choices` property)
- `'positional'`: Non-option argument by position
- `'custom'`: Custom parsing with user-defined `parse` function

members.name: short
members.description:
Single character alias for the long option name.

As example, allows users to use `-x` instead of `--extended-option`.
Only valid for non-positional argument types.

members.name: description
members.description:
Human-readable description of the argument's purpose.

Used for help text generation and documentation.
Should be concise but descriptive enough to understand the argument's role.

members.name: required
members.description:
Marks the argument as required.

When `true`, the argument must be provided by the user.
If missing, an `ArgResolveError` with type 'required' will be thrown.

Note: Only `true` is allowed (not `false`) to make intent explicit.

members.name: multiple
members.description:
Allows the argument to accept multiple values.

When `true`, the resolved value becomes an array.
For options: can be specified multiple times (--tag foo --tag bar)
For positional: collects remaining positional arguments

Note: Only `true` is allowed (not `false`) to make intent explicit.

members.name: negatable
members.description:
Enables negation for boolean arguments using `--no-` prefix.

When `true`, allows users to explicitly set the boolean to `false`
using `--no-option-name`. When `false` or omitted, only positive
form is available.

Only applicable to `type: 'boolean'` arguments.

members.name: choices
members.description:
Array of allowed string values for enum-type arguments.

Required when `type: 'enum'`. The argument value must be one of these choices,
otherwise an `ArgResolveError` with type 'type' will be thrown.

Supports both mutable arrays and readonly arrays for type safety.

members.name: default
members.description:
Default value used when the argument is not provided.

The type must match the argument's `type` property:
- `string` type: string default
- `boolean` type: boolean default
- `number` type: number default
- `enum` type: must be one of the `choices` values
- `positional`/`custom` type: any appropriate default

members.name: toKebab
members.description:
Converts the argument name from camelCase to kebab-case for CLI usage.

When `true`, a property like `maxCount` becomes available as `--max-count`.
This allows [CAC](https://github.com/cacjs/cac) user-friendly property names while maintaining CLI conventions.

Can be overridden globally with `resolveArgs({ toKebab: true })`.

Note: Only `true` is allowed (not `false`) to make intent explicit.

members.name: conflicts
members.description:
Names of other options that conflict with this option.

When this option is used together with any of the conflicting options,
an `ArgResolveError` with type 'conflict' will be thrown.

Conflicts only need to be defined on one side - if option A defines a conflict
with option B, the conflict is automatically detected when both are used,
regardless of whether B also defines a conflict with A.

Supports both single option name or array of option names.
Option names must match the property keys in the schema object exactly
(no automatic conversion between camelCase and kebab-case).

members.name: metavar
members.description:
Display name hint for help text generation.

Provides a meaningful type hint for the argument value in help output.
Particularly useful for `type: 'custom'` arguments where the type
name would otherwise be unhelpful.

members.name: parse
members.description:
Custom parsing function for `type: 'custom'` arguments.

Required when `type: 'custom'`. Receives the raw string value and must
return the parsed result. Should throw an Error (or subclass) if parsing fails.

The function's return type becomes the resolved argument type.

params.description: Raw string value from command line

returns.description: Parsed value of any type

entries.name: Args
entries.description:
An object that contains {@link ArgSchema | argument schema}.

This type is used to define the structure and validation rules for command line arguments.
entries.signature: interface Args

entries.name: ArgSchema
entries.description:
An argument schema definition for command-line argument parsing.

This schema is similar to the schema of Node.js `util.parseArgs` but with extended features:
- Additional `required` and `description` properties
- Extended `type` support: 'string', 'boolean', 'number', 'enum', 'positional', 'custom'
- Simplified `default` property (single type, not union types)
examples:
Basic string argument:
```ts
const schema: ArgSchema = {
  type: 'string',
  description: 'Server hostname',
  default: 'localhost'
}
```

Required number argument with alias:
```ts
const schema: ArgSchema = {
  type: 'number',
  short: 'p',
  description: 'Port number to listen on',
  required: true
}
```

Enum argument with choices:
```ts
const schema: ArgSchema = {
  type: 'enum',
  choices: ['info', 'warn', 'error'],
  description: 'Logging level',
  default: 'info'
}
```

members.name: type
members.description:
Type of the argument value.

- `'string'`: Text value (default if not specified)
- `'boolean'`: `true`/`false` flag (can be negatable with `--no-` prefix)
- `'number'`: Numeric value (parsed as integer or float)
- `'enum'`: One of predefined string values (requires `choices` property)
- `'positional'`: Non-option argument by position
- `'custom'`: Custom parsing with user-defined `parse` function

members.name: short
members.description:
Single character alias for the long option name.

As example, allows users to use `-x` instead of `--extended-option`.
Only valid for non-positional argument types.

members.name: description
members.description:
Human-readable description of the argument's purpose.

Used for help text generation and documentation.
Should be concise but descriptive enough to understand the argument's role.

members.name: required
members.description:
Marks the argument as required.

When `true`, the argument must be provided by the user.
If missing, an `ArgResolveError` with type 'required' will be thrown.

Note: Only `true` is allowed (not `false`) to make intent explicit.

members.name: multiple
members.description:
Allows the argument to accept multiple values.

When `true`, the resolved value becomes an array.
For options: can be specified multiple times (--tag foo --tag bar)
For positional: collects remaining positional arguments

Note: Only `true` is allowed (not `false`) to make intent explicit.

members.name: negatable
members.description:
Enables negation for boolean arguments using `--no-` prefix.

When `true`, allows users to explicitly set the boolean to `false`
using `--no-option-name`. When `false` or omitted, only positive
form is available.

Only applicable to `type: 'boolean'` arguments.

members.name: choices
members.description:
Array of allowed string values for enum-type arguments.

Required when `type: 'enum'`. The argument value must be one of these choices,
otherwise an `ArgResolveError` with type 'type' will be thrown.

Supports both mutable arrays and readonly arrays for type safety.

members.name: default
members.description:
Default value used when the argument is not provided.

The type must match the argument's `type` property:
- `string` type: string default
- `boolean` type: boolean default
- `number` type: number default
- `enum` type: must be one of the `choices` values
- `positional`/`custom` type: any appropriate default

members.name: toKebab
members.description:
Converts the argument name from camelCase to kebab-case for CLI usage.

When `true`, a property like `maxCount` becomes available as `--max-count`.
This allows [CAC](https://github.com/cacjs/cac) user-friendly property names while maintaining CLI conventions.

Can be overridden globally with `resolveArgs({ toKebab: true })`.

Note: Only `true` is allowed (not `false`) to make intent explicit.

members.name: conflicts
members.description:
Names of other options that conflict with this option.

When this option is used together with any of the conflicting options,
an `ArgResolveError` with type 'conflict' will be thrown.

Conflicts only need to be defined on one side - if option A defines a conflict
with option B, the conflict is automatically detected when both are used,
regardless of whether B also defines a conflict with A.

Supports both single option name or array of option names.
Option names must match the property keys in the schema object exactly
(no automatic conversion between camelCase and kebab-case).

members.name: metavar
members.description:
Display name hint for help text generation.

Provides a meaningful type hint for the argument value in help output.
Particularly useful for `type: 'custom'` arguments where the type
name would otherwise be unhelpful.

members.name: parse
members.description:
Custom parsing function for `type: 'custom'` arguments.

Required when `type: 'custom'`. Receives the raw string value and must
return the parsed result. Should throw an Error (or subclass) if parsing fails.

The function's return type becomes the resolved argument type.

params.name: value
params.description: Raw string value from command line

returns.description: Parsed value of any type

tags.throws: Error or subclass when value is invalid

members.name: explicit
members.description:
Whether arguments were explicitly provided by the user.

- `true`: The argument was explicitly provided via command line
- `false`: The argument was not explicitly provided. This means either:
  - The value comes from a default value defined in the argument schema
  - The value is `undefined` (no explicit input and no default value)

members.name: values
members.description:
Command values, that is the values of the command that is executed.
Resolve values with `resolveArgs` from command arguments and {@linkcode Command.args}.

members.name: positionals
members.description:
Command positionals arguments, that is the positionals of the command that is executed.
Resolve positionals with `resolveArgs` from command arguments.

members.name: rest
members.description: Command rest arguments, that is the remaining argument not resolved by the optional command option delimiter `--`.

Original command line arguments.
This argument is passed from `cli` function.

members.name: tokens
members.description: Argument tokens, that is parsed by `parseArgs` function.

members.name: omitted
members.description: Whether the currently executing command has been executed with the sub-command name omitted.

members.name: callMode
members.description:
Command call mode.
The command call mode is `entry` when the command is executed as an entry command, and `subCommand` when the command is executed as a sub-command.

members.name: commandPath
members.description:
The path of nested sub-commands that were resolved to reach the current command.

For example, if the user runs `git remote add`, `commandPath` would be `['remote', 'add']`.
For the entry command, this is an empty array.

members.name: toKebab
members.description:
Whether to convert the camel-case style argument name to kebab-case.
This context value is set from {@linkcode Command.toKebab} option.

members.name: log
members.description:
Output a message.

If {@linkcode CommandEnvironment.usageSilent} is true, the message is not output.

members.description: Command context extensions.

members.name: validationError
members.description:
Validation error from argument parsing.
This will be set if argument validation fails during CLI execution.

modules.description:
Parser combinator entry point.

This entry point exports composable combinator factory functions for building
type-safe argument schemas. These combinators produce {@link ArgSchema} objects
that can be used anywhere regular argument schemas are accepted.

entries.name: args
entries.description:
Type-safe schema factory.

Returns the input unchanged at runtime, but provides type inference
so that `satisfies Args` is not needed.

typeParameters.name: T
typeParameters.description: The exact schema type.

returns.description: The same schema object with its type inferred.

entries.signature: declare function args<T extends Args>(fields: T): T

entries.name: boolean
entries.description:
Create a boolean argument schema.

Boolean arguments are existence-based. The resolver passes `"true"` or `"false"`
to the parse function based on the presence or negation of the flag.

returns.description: A combinator schema for boolean flags.

entries.signature: declare function boolean(opts?: BooleanOptions): CombinatorSchema<boolean>

entries.name: choice
entries.description:
Create an enum-like argument schema with literal type inference.

Uses `const T` generic to infer literal union types from the values array.

typeParameters.name: T
typeParameters.description: The readonly array of allowed string values.

returns.description: A combinator schema that resolves to a union of the allowed values.

entries.signature: declare function choice<const T extends readonly string[]>(values: T, opts?: BaseOptions): CombinatorSchema<T[number]>

entries.name: combinator
entries.description:
Create a custom argument schema with a user-defined parse function.

This is the most general custom combinator. Use it when none of the built-in
base combinators ({@link string}, {@link number}, {@link integer},
{@link float}, {@link boolean}, {@link choice}) fit your needs.

The returned schema has `type: 'custom'`.

typeParameters.name: T
typeParameters.description: The parsed value type.

params.name: config
params.description: Configuration with a parse function and optional metavar.

returns.description: A combinator schema that resolves to the parse function's return type.

entries.signature: declare function combinator<T>(config: CombinatorOptions<T>): CombinatorSchema<T>

entries.name: describe
entries.description:
Set a description on a combinator schema for help text generation.

The original schema is not modified.

typeParameters.name: T
typeParameters.description: The schema's parsed type.

typeParameters.name: D
typeParameters.description: The description string literal type.

returns.description: A new schema with the description set.

entries.signature: declare function describe<T, D extends string>(schema: CombinatorSchema<T>, text: D): CombinatorSchema<T> & CombinatorDescribe<D>

entries.name: extend
entries.description:
Extend a schema by overriding or adding fields.

Equivalent to `merge(base, overrides)` but communicates the intent of
intentional overrides rather than general composition.

typeParameters.name: T
typeParameters.description: Base schema type.

typeParameters.name: U
typeParameters.description: Overrides schema type.

returns.description: A new schema with overrides applied.

entries.signature: declare function extend<T extends Args, U extends Args>(base: T, overrides: U): Omit<T, keyof U> & U

entries.name: float
entries.description:
Create a floating-point argument schema with optional range validation.

Rejects `NaN` and `Infinity` values.

returns.description: A combinator schema that resolves to number (float).

entries.signature: declare function float(opts?: FloatOptions): CombinatorSchema<number>

Only accepts integer values (no decimals).

returns.description: A combinator schema that resolves to number (integer).

entries.signature: declare function integer(opts?: IntegerOptions): CombinatorSchema<number>

The original schema is not modified.

returns.description: A merged schema containing all fields.
